mnemonics.title: DP Table Filling Order
mnemonics.content: Remember: BASE CASE FIRST - Fill row 0 and column 0 with zeros. Then fill cell by cell, left to right, top to bottom.

mnemonics.title: 1D Array Optimization
mnemonics.content: RIGHT TO LEFT - When using 1D array, iterate capacity from W down to weight[i] to avoid using same item twice.

mnemonics.title: Greedy for Fractional
mnemonics.content: RATIO = Value ÷ Weight - Sort by RATIO descending, pick largest ratios first.

acronyms.usage: Algorithm design technique using optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems
Each item either taken completely or not at all
Items can be taken in fractions

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a grid: rows = items (0 to n), columns = weights (0 to W). Each cell (i,w) = max value using first i items with capacity w.
For each cell (i,w): take MAX of (skip item: go to cell above) OR (take item: value at top + current item value)
Think of single row being updated. Going right-to-left ensures each item contributes only once per solution.
Picture items ranked by value-per-weight like students ranked by height. Tallest (best ratio) picked first.

analogies.analogy: Like packing a suitcase for a trip where you can only take whole items (complete clothes, not half a shirt). Must maximize utility within weight limit.
Like filling a jar with gold dust and coins - you can take portions. Pick most valuable (highest value/weight) first.
Like a student keeping a formula sheet - rather than rederive formulas, look up previously solved similar problems.
Like finding best route to destination - best route via intermediate point = best route to that point + best route from there.
